brief_summary: Time-vs-satisfaction chart with two diverging curves. Teal: 'test-optimal' build peaks sharply at the 60-second mark and then drops away through 30 min, 2 hours, 4 hours, workday-end. Warm-grey: 'daily-driver' build starts a step lower but plateaus and holds across the day. A dashed teal vertical line marks where the typing test ends. Faint teal fill emphasizes the widening gap between the curves.
data_sources: typing-tests-lie.mdx § 'Tests don't punish micro-soreness' — 'A 67g spring on a long-pole linear feels punchy in a one-minute sprint and feels like a forearm complaint by 4 PM on a Wednesday'

typing-tests-lie.mdx § 'Tests time short bursts and hide endurance fade' — 'A sixty-second test ends before the typist's typing posture has degraded ... A keyboard that slowly degrades the typist's posture across an hour will return identical numbers to a keyboard that doesn't, on the test.'
